title: Coelia triptera
taxonomy: Coelia triptera is classified as a species within the genus Coelia, which belongs to the Orchidaceae family. This family is commonly known as the orchid family, comprising one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The Coelia genus is a small group of orchids, and Coelia triptera is one of its species. Coelia triptera is native to Central and South America. It is a terrestrial orchid that thrives in warm, humid climates.

intro: Coelia triptera, also known as the "Queen's Wreath," is a rare and exotic orchid species found in tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Coelia triptera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
